title: Banks Shot

jokes.text: He's not allowed to use the car again until August of 1992.
jokes.setup: Listing who can use the car - Ashley for birthday party, Carlton for glee club
jokes.punchline: Will is banned for an absurdly long time (over a year)
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation joke with good character work - establishes Will as trouble-prone and the family's exasperation with his car misuse

jokes.text: What is it this time, Hilary? I've started recycling.
jokes.setup: Hilary says she's very offended, setting up for something serious
jokes.punchline: Vivian assumes it's about recycling (something trivial)
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Vivian's familiarity with Hilary's dramatics, though relatively mild

jokes.text: After all, in a couple of years, I'm going to have my own family and my own butler.
jokes.setup: Hilary wants to learn household responsibility for her future
jokes.punchline: She assumes having a butler is normal for everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character comedy - showcases her privileged worldview and obliviousness

jokes.text: I hope it's no one I know.
jokes.setup: Hilary talking about getting her own butler
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry concern about whoever gets stuck with her
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line - perfect deadpan delivery implied, economical, and character-perfect

jokes.text: That's why. How y'all doing? I just got my hair cut. Kind of fly, huh?
jokes.setup: Philip asked why Will doesn't work from home
jokes.punchline: Will's loud, distracting entrance proves the point immediately
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff structure - Philip's question gets answered immediately by Will's disruptive entrance, perfect timing

jokes.text: But, I guess y'all got kind of a reverse thing going on here.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his haircut style versus the family's
jokes.punchline: Noting Carlton has hair on sides, none on top (balding joke)
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about hair/class differences, though fairly standard material

jokes.text: Finally. I always thought you could do better. Good luck.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she's leaving
jokes.punchline: Will thinks she's leaving Philip permanently and approves
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - audience and characters think one thing, Will interprets completely differently and supports it

jokes.text: Don't leave me here with him! He's going to eat me. Don't leave me here.
jokes.setup: Will realizes Aunt Viv is just going on a trip
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top physical plea including literal fear of being eaten
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat with Will's characteristic over-dramatization

jokes.text: And I just took a multivitamin.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing romantic opportunities - parents away, butler off
jokes.punchline: His idea of preparation is a multivitamin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character comedy - innocent, dorky, completely missing the point of romantic preparation

jokes.text: I think you're the most talented member on the swimnastics team.
jokes.setup: Carlton is trying to impress Michelle romantically
jokes.punchline: He coins the ridiculous portmanteau 'swimnastics'
jokes.explanation: Carlton's attempt at sophisticated conversation backfires with a clumsy made-up word

jokes.text: I can hold my breath for long periods of time.
jokes.setup: Carlton complimenting Michelle on swimnastics
jokes.punchline: His flirtation is about breath-holding ability
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton awkwardness - his attempt at seduction is hilariously practical

jokes.text: Good. We're going under.
jokes.setup: Michelle's breath-holding comment
jokes.punchline: Carlton's literal interpretation for romantic purposes
jokes.explanation: Continues Carlton's awkward seduction with economical delivery

jokes.text: Hello. Rice Krispie Treats?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to seduce Michelle
jokes.punchline: Hilary interrupts with innocent snacks
jokes.explanation: Good comedic interruption - innocent treats contrasting with romantic tension

jokes.text: remember, you don't have to pet to be popular.
jokes.setup: Hilary giving Carlton and Michelle a serious talk about teenage feelings
jokes.punchline: Her advice is hilariously innocent/outdated
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary character moment - trying to be responsible but hilariously out of touch

jokes.text: Let's go to the mall.
jokes.setup: Hilary's awkward sex talk
jokes.punchline: Carlton's immediate deflection to the mall
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Carlton's discomfort and escape attempt

jokes.text: That's right, Will. No one can hear you scream.
jokes.setup: Will calling for Aunt Viv after she's gone
jokes.punchline: Philip's ominous horror movie reference
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor from Philip - unexpected reference showing his menacing side

jokes.text: That's not true. I have my wine collection.
jokes.setup: Will accusing Philip of only finding pleasure in Will's unhappiness
jokes.punchline: Philip dryly admitting to another source of pleasure
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan from Philip - doesn't deny the accusation, just adds wine as another pleasure

jokes.text: He said he ain't want you to go to the pool hall. It's a difference.
jokes.setup: Will saying Uncle Phil said he couldn't go
jokes.punchline: Fred's semantic distinction between 'can't' and 'don't want you to'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay showing street-smart logic finding loopholes in authority

jokes.text: What are you, ignorant? Pick up the damn keys.
jokes.setup: Fred explaining the semantic difference
jokes.punchline: His impatience with Will not getting it
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Fred's street wisdom and impatience

jokes.text: And next time you come in here, I'm going to use this.
jokes.setup: Owner kicking someone out
jokes.punchline: Threatens with pool cue (implied visual)
jokes.explanation: Standard tough-guy intimidation, fairly predictable

jokes.text: Fred, get up! I can't get no sleep around here today to save my life.
jokes.setup: Will asking for table seven
jokes.punchline: Fred is sleeping on the pool table
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag establishing the rough nature of the pool hall

jokes.text: No spitting, no fighting, and use coasters.
jokes.setup: Listing serious pool hall rules
jokes.punchline: Third rule is prissy and domestic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - first two rules are tough, third is unexpectedly domestic

jokes.text: What the hey. Maybe just $5 a ball.
jokes.setup: Will initially saying just playing for fun
jokes.punchline: Gets drawn into gambling anyway
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Will getting in over his head, predictable character beat

jokes.text: Ain't no thing but a chicken wing. My game is all that.
jokes.setup: Challenger asking if Will wants to play the big boys
jokes.punchline: Will's cocky slang response
jokes.explanation: Classic Will catchphrase delivery, good character voice but fairly standard

jokes.text: He also said that if it weren't for the fact that he had won so much from that fool over there, he'd take your money, too.
jokes.setup: Fred 'translating' Will's meaning about not wanting to embarrass the opponent
jokes.punchline: Fred escalates Will's boast beyond what he intended
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Fred's 'help' makes Will's situation worse

jokes.text: Hustled is such an ugly word. I prefer to think of it as creative money management.
jokes.setup: Will accusing them of hustling him
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reframing of hustling
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing the hustler's smooth talking and euphemistic language

jokes.text: And if all your friends jumped off a cliff, would you do it, too?
jokes.setup: Ashley saying all her friends will be at the party
jokes.punchline: Hilary uses the most cliched parent response
jokes.explanation: Standard joke about cliched parenting - works because it shows Hilary's inexperience

jokes.text: And no running in the house. You'll put your eye out.
jokes.setup: Hilary trying to be authoritative with Ashley
jokes.punchline: Completely random safety warning
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Hilary grasping at parental authority with random warnings

jokes.text: You blew $300 and you don't even have a sweater to show for it.
jokes.setup: Will explaining he lost $300 gambling
jokes.punchline: Hilary's shopping-focused perspective on the loss
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary character comedy - reduces gambling loss to shopping terms she understands

jokes.text: That's what I always do.
jokes.setup: Will saying he'll borrow money from his butler
jokes.punchline: Carlton casually admits this is his normal practice
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton privilege moment - casually admits to exploiting Geoffrey

jokes.text: So you can fix me a Rob Roy with a Sidecar.
jokes.setup: Fred mistaking Geoffrey for the butler
jokes.punchline: Orders specific cocktails from what he thinks is the help
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash - Fred's street background meeting perceived high-class service

jokes.text: Man, it is hard to find good help these days.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey telling Fred to get out
jokes.punchline: Fred's ironic comment about service quality
jokes.explanation: Good ironic delivery from Fred missing the point of why he was rejected

jokes.text: I see you've met my Earth Science teacher.
jokes.setup: Philip encountering the threatening Fred
jokes.punchline: Will's desperate lie about Fred's identity
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Will's quick thinking under pressure

jokes.text: They got guys in there that make you look like Webster.
jokes.setup: Will warning Philip about the dangerous pool hall guys
jokes.punchline: Comparing large Philip to tiny TV character Webster
jokes.explanation: Great visual comparison joke - Philip is large, Webster was tiny, absurd escalation

jokes.text: In the early '80s, I was sparring butler for Mr. Chuck Norris.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offering to accompany Philip to deal with hooligans
jokes.punchline: Reveals unexpectedly badass martial arts background
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey moment - reveals hidden badass past with perfect specificity (Chuck Norris)

jokes.text: That's where we got you fooled. We ain't got no license.
jokes.setup: Philip threatening legal action and license revocation
jokes.punchline: Pool hall operates completely illegally anyway
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Philip's legal threats - completely undermines his professional approach

jokes.text: The only legal phrase these people know is, 'Will the defendant please rise.'
jokes.setup: Philip trying legal intimidation
jokes.punchline: Will noting their criminal background experience
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the clientele's legal experience from the defendant side

jokes.text: I've seen it on TV.
jokes.setup: Philip confidently saying pool is easy, just putting balls in holes
jokes.punchline: His only experience is watching television
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Philip's overconfidence - reveals his complete inexperience

jokes.text: Geoffrey, get me one of those stick thingies.
jokes.setup: Philip decides to play pool
jokes.punchline: He doesn't even know what a pool cue is called
jokes.explanation: Philip's upper-class unfamiliarity with pool terminology reinforces his fish-out-of-water status

jokes.text: At least he's got the terminology down.
jokes.setup: Philip asking for 'stick thingies'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic assessment of Philip's pool knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan - sarcastically praising Philip's obvious ignorance

jokes.text: Don't be ridiculous, Geoffrey. It's simple geometry.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey questioning Philip's pool playing wisdom
jokes.punchline: Philip's overconfident academic approach to pool
jokes.explanation: Good Philip character moment - applies academic thinking to practical skill

jokes.text: That was just a practice shot.
jokes.setup: Philip presumably missing badly on his first attempt
jokes.punchline: His excuse for obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Standard 'practice shot' excuse, predictable but fits Philip's character

jokes.text: No, I think I'm getting a lot better, don't you?
jokes.setup: Philip owing $400 after getting worse
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Standard delusion joke, works for Philip's stubborn character but not very original

jokes.text: Much better. That's not how I see it. You still stink.
jokes.setup: Philip asking if he's getting better
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcasm followed by hustler's blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Will's sarcasm and hustler's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Geoffrey? Break out Lucille.
jokes.setup: Philip agreeing to another game at higher stakes
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has a named pool cue
jokes.explanation: Excellent twist - reveals Philip was hustling them all along, perfect setup/payoff with the named cue

jokes.text: And 10% for his manager.
jokes.setup: Philip winning money back
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey claiming manager's fee (callback to earlier hustling)
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the earlier manager's fee joke, shows Geoffrey's quick thinking

jokes.text: after you done had me up in your house.
jokes.setup: Fred being betrayed after the hustle reversal
jokes.punchline: His complaint about hospitality after trying to rob them
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Fred complaining about manners after attempted extortion

jokes.text: I handled it, I prevailed, and now I think I'm ready to have children of my own.
jokes.setup: Hilary proudly claiming she handled weekend responsibility well
jokes.punchline: About to be contradicted by the actual children
jokes.explanation: Good setup for immediate contradiction, shows Hilary's delusion

jokes.text: Hilary was a tyrant all weekend. She was terrible.
jokes.setup: Hilary's proud claim of success
jokes.punchline: Children immediately contradicting with harsh judgment
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Hilary's delusion, standard but effective structure

jokes.text: Next time you go out of town, I beg of you, please just put food and water in a dish.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about Hilary's tyranny
jokes.punchline: Comparing their treatment to pets
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - preferring pet treatment to Hilary's supervision

jokes.text: Do you think she'll put two and two together when the cops dig my body up.
jokes.setup: Philip explains why he didn't tell Vivian about the pool hall incident
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically suggests Philip will murder him
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to earlier horror movie references but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: What, you're going to spank me? You know the severity of what you did tonight.
jokes.setup: Philip quoting 'hard head makes soft behind'
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation about spanking, Philip's serious response
jokes.explanation: Standard joke about punishment threats, reasonable character moment

jokes.text: You know, a hard head makes for a soft behind.
jokes.setup: Philip is lecturing Will about being hard-headed
jokes.punchline: He uses an old-fashioned saying about spanking consequences
jokes.explanation: Traditional parental wisdom that fits Philip's character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: Your money? I'll play you for it.
jokes.setup: Will thanking Philip for winning 'his' money back
jokes.punchline: Philip claiming it was his money, Will immediately suggesting gambling again
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat - shows Will hasn't learned his lesson about gambling

tmdb_overview: Will crawls to Philip for help after sneaking out on the town and getting hustled by sleazy pool sharks.
